rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY ROMAN SR. et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Scott Technologies, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 4 and off calendar as to issue 5. Mr. Roman¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Dr. Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Roman ¿ whether defendant knew or should have known of the hazards of asbestos exposure. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant failed to sustain its burden on the issue of whether defendant knew or should have known whether the materials attributable to defendant to which Mr. Roman was allegedly exposed contained asbestos. Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS et al VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion for order granting leave to file third amended complaint is granted in part and denied in part. It is denied as to the proposed claim based on the May 2009 asbestosis diagnosis as Mr. Ross had a prior action based on that claim. It is granted as to all other aspects, as all other aspects of plaintiffs¿ motion were unopposed. Pursuant to C.C.P. 473, plaintiffs may amend their pleading as stated by this ruling. Once filed, the third amended complaint will be deemed served on all defendants who have previously appeared. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt For Lita Olquin'S Failure To Appear At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Order to show cause re: contempt for Lita Olquin¿s failure to appear at deposition pursuant to a valid subpoena is off calendar. Counsel for John K. Bice sufficiently demonstrated that Ms. Olquin passed away. Per the court¿s April 11 order, the order to show cause thus comes off calendar. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA BROWNSON, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS VS. JOHN CRANE, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s demurrer: Defendant¿s general demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity, if she is able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co. Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant¿s demurrer to the survival causes of action is continued to May 24 to be heard following the court¿s ruling on plaintiff¿s motion to consolidate this action (no. 275998) with the prior action (no. 275773). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court.
